##  Time gated Superconducting Nanowire Single Photon Detectors 
Enhance your signal-to-noise ratio with high precision filtering
Go to
  * [ Basic principle ](https://www.singlequantum.com/technology/gated-snspds/#section-number-3)
  * [ Fast-gating solution ](https://www.singlequantum.com/technology/gated-snspds/#section-number-4)


Questions?
[ Talk to us ](https://www.singlequantum.com/contact/)
##  Fast detector switch 
In applications where background noise reduction and temporal filtering are crucial, the ability to swiftly activate and deactivate photon detectors is often essential.
Additionally, in experiments involving resonant excitation and collection, gating can be transformative for filtering out undesired photons, eliminating the need for cross-polarization techniques and their associated losses. For example, this approach is particularly advantageous in applications such as fluorescence spectroscopy, single-photon LiDAR, quantum communication, telecommunications, and microscopy.
![](https://cdn.shortpixel.ai/spai/q_glossy+w_492+to_auto+ret_img/www.singlequantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/RENDERS-1.png)
![](https://www.singlequantum.com/technology/gated-snspds/)
###### Highlights
  * Fast turn on/off time in just 5-20 ns
  * Temporal Filtering of Laser Excitation
  * Laser Suppression up to 50 dB
  * Add-on to the external driver unit
  * Adjustable gating windows 
  * Enhanced Signal-to-Noise Ratio
  * Noise suppression – detect only photons that matter


##### Fast-gating technology
At Single Quantum, we’ve developed fast-gated SNSPDs capable of restoring full detection efficiency within just 5–20 nanoseconds. Our detectors operate using an externally clocked gate signal, allowing precise control — you can open and close the detector exactly when needed. Due to the inductive properties of the SNSPD, turn-on is not instantaneous; the time to reach full efficiency defines the minimum recovery time for gated experiments. Contact us to know more and for customized solutions!
![Myimage 1](https://www.singlequantum.com/technology/gated-snspds/)
###### measured at 1310 nm
[Contact us](https://www.singlequantum.com/contact/)
##  The fastest solution on the market 
Single Quantum offers a range of gating speeds, coupled with high efficiency and minimal timing jitter, to effectively address the physical limitations of SNSPDs.

            <h2 class="c-heading c-header__heading heading-four ">
        Fast detector switch

    </h2>
            </header>

                
                <div class="c-content  s-content-media__content">
        <p>In applications where background noise reduction and temporal filtering are crucial, the ability to swiftly activate and deactivate photon detectors is often essential.</p>
<p>Additionally, in experiments involving resonant excitation and collection, gating can be transformative for filtering out undesired photons, eliminating the need for cross-polarization techniques and their associated losses. For example, this approach is particularly advantageous in applications such as fluorescence spectroscopy, single-photon LiDAR, quantum communication, telecommunications, and microscopy.</p>

    </div>

<ul class="list-one">
<li><span data-teams="true">Fast turn on/off time </span>in just 5-20 ns</li>
<li>Temporal Filtering of Laser Excitation</li>

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        </div>
            <div class="col-12 col-md-7 content-right  fade slide-up">
                                                                                                        <div class="c-content ">
        <h5>Fast-gating technology</h5>
<p>At Single Quantum, we’ve developed fast-gated SNSPDs capable of restoring full detection efficiency within just 5–20 nanoseconds. Our detectors operate using an externally clocked gate signal, allowing precise control — you can open and close the detector exactly when needed. Due to the inductive properties of the SNSPD, turn-on is not instantaneous; the time to reach full efficiency defines the minimum recovery time for gated experiments. <span data-teams="true">Contact us to know more and for customized solutions!</span></p>

    </div>

	let consented = [];
	for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(consent)) {
		if (value === 'granted') {
			consented.push(key);
		}
	}
	cmplz_set_cookie('cmplz_consent_mode', consented.join(','), false );
	consentListeners.forEach((callback) => {
		callback(consent);
	});
});
						</script>  


</body></html>
